bugs.summary: The proposed patch blocks foreach expansion when item count equals maxItems.
bugs.impact: `maxItems` is intended as an inclusive upper bound. Using `>=` rejects valid workflows with exactly the configured maximum number of items.
acceptableFixes: Restore the runtime guard to `items.length > stage.maxItems`.
Restore the runtime guard and add an exact-boundary regression test where exactly `maxItems` items are allowed.
mustNotSay: `maxItems` is intended to be an exclusive upper bound.
Adding a boundary test alone fixes the runtime regression.

mustAvoidClaims: Using prior A/B run notes as evidence.
Relying on git history instead of the sanitized workspace and proposed patch.

difficulty.rationale: Boundary-condition task; candidates must reason about inclusive maxItems semantics.

approval.notes: Locked after revised gold evidence, explicit RED/GREEN repro, and final Kimi low approval. Earlier GPT/Kimi reviews confirmed bug existence but requested evidence revisions.
